too often I see packages broken by patch versions of Julia, but it's one of those cases :(
As a specific example, `using UnionCollections, Dictionaries, FlexiMaps` throws `ERROR: LoadError: type Nothing has no field UnionDictionary` on this line: https://github.com/JuliaAPlavin/UnionCollections.jl/blob/0000000018ecd297a2e7448671da83c035b3da4c/ext/FlexiMapsDictionariesExt.jl#L8.

Effectively, it does `get_extension(MyPackage, :WeakDepA)` in the extension for WeakDepA + WeakDepB.
Judging by the documentation, it should work:
-  `get_extension` returns the extension if it's loaded
> get_extension(parent::Module, extension::Symbol)
> Return the module for extension of parent or return nothing if the extension is not loaded.

- Pkg docs say the following about extension loading:
> A package extension is a module in a file (similar to a package) that is automatically loaded when some other set of packages are loaded into the Julia session.

In the specific case with UnionCollections, this is what I expect to happen on that `using` line:
1. UnionCollections is loaded
2. Dictionaries is loaded
3. UnionCollections -> DictionariesExt is loaded (following Pkg docs)
4. FlexiMaps is loaded
5. UnionCollections -> FlexiMapsDictionariesExt is loaded, it sees DictionariesExt loaded already and successfully imports that (following get_extension docs)

And that's indeed how the observable behavior worked on earlier Julia versions, including 1.11.0. Unfortunately, the fix appeared to stop working in the 1.11.2 release.

Maybe someone could help explain the proper workflow?

Concretely, our package Sunny now has in its Project.toml:

```
[extensions]
GLMakiePrecompilesExt = ["Makie", "GLMakie"]
PlottingExt = "Makie"
```

My understanding is that this would allow GLMakiePrecompilesExt to depend on methods in PlottingExt. GLMakiePrecompilesExt contains the following code:

```jl
import Sunny, GLMakie
import PrecompileTools as PT

PT.@setup_workload begin
    PT.@compile_workload begin
        # This specific method is defined in PlottingExt
        Sunny.view_crystal(...)
    end
end
```

Somewhere between the merge of #56228 and the release of 1.11.2, this workflow seemed to break. The error is:

```julia> using Sunny, GLMakie
Precompiling PlottingExt...
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 8 seconds. 322 already precompiled.
Precompiling GLMakiePrecompilesExt...
Info Given GLMakiePrecompilesExt was explicitly requested, output will be shown live 
ERROR: LoadError: MethodError: no method matching view_crystal(::Sunny.Crystal)
The function `view_crystal` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.
```

This means that somehow GLMakiePrecompilesExt is not actually seeing the methods defined in PlottingExt. Any ideas?

There is a tridiagonal eigensolver inside
every dense sym eigensolver?
don't tell me i'm getting unwanted eigenvectors too or something ....

a sanity check is that for n large -- doubling n would take 
8 x the time for the dense method,  and perhaps 4 x the time
for the tridiagonal method, when compared to the original n.

More importantly, the tridiagonal should be strictly less time
than the dense as the tridiagonal is inside the dense.
At least that's the theory

``` julia
julia> n=1000;a=randn(n,n);s=a+a'; @time eigvals(s);
elapsed time: 0.357638326 seconds

julia> n=1000;a=randn(n,n);s=a+a';d=diag(s);e=diag(s,1); @time eigvals(SymTridiagonal(d,e));
elapsed time: 0.766760059 seconds
```

I see that `@time eig` is a bit more reasonable but somehow i doubt this is `dsteqr` time.

Here is how to run updated benchmarks:

```jl
using LinearAlgebra
n = 5000
d=randn(n); e=randn(n-1); T=SymTridiagonal(d,e);

# Eigenvalues only
@time(eigvals(T)); # 0.3 s, calls LAPACK.stev!
@time LAPACK.stegr!('N', 'A', copy(d), copy(e), 0.0, 0.0, 0, 0); # 0.5 s
@time LAPACK.stebz!('A', 'E', 0.0, 0.0, 0, 0, 1e-5, d, e); # 1.4 s

# Eigenvalues and eigenvectors
@time(eigen(T)); # 2.1 s
@time LAPACK.stegr!('V', 'A', copy(d), copy(e), 0.0, 0.0, 0, 0); # 2.1s

# Looks like https://netlib.org/lapack/explore-html-3.4.2/dc/d9d/sstemr_8f.html
# not wrapped? (RRRs, Relatively Robust Representations).
```

Curiously, I couldn't find a Julia LAPACK wrapper to [`sstemr`](https://netlib.org/lapack/explore-html-3.4.2/dc/d9d/sstemr_8f.html), an $O(N^2)$ algorithm based on RRRs, Relatively Robust Representations. The Netlib docs don't say much about the difference between `sstemr` and [`sstegr`](https://netlib.org/lapack/explore-html-3.4.2/d6/d0e/sstegr_8f.html), which is the one used in [`LinearAlgebra/src/tridiag.jl`](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/c31710a7d93c84b0e1f79c7d9c7ba7bca948ba10/stdlib/LinearAlgebra/src/tridiag.jl#L274).

As a specific example, `using UnionCollections, Dictionaries, FlexiMaps` throws `ERROR: LoadError: type Nothing has no field UnionDictionary` on this line: https://github.com/JuliaAPlavin/UnionCollections.jl/blob/0000000018ecd297a2e7448671da83c035b3da4c/ext/FlexiMapsDictionariesExt.jl#L8.

Effectively, it does `get_extension(MyPackage, :WeakDepA)` in the extension for WeakDepA + WeakDepB.
Judging by the documentation, it should work:
-  `get_extension` returns the extension if it's loaded
> get_extension(parent::Module, extension::Symbol)
> Return the module for extension of parent or return nothing if the extension is not loaded.

- Pkg docs say the following about extension loading:
> A package extension is a module in a file (similar to a package) that is automatically loaded when some other set of packages are loaded into the Julia session.

In the specific case with UnionCollections, this is what I expect to happen on that `using` line:
1. UnionCollections is loaded
2. Dictionaries is loaded
3. UnionCollections -> DictionariesExt is loaded (following Pkg docs)
4. FlexiMaps is loaded
5. UnionCollections -> FlexiMapsDictionariesExt is loaded, it sees DictionariesExt loaded already and successfully imports that (following get_extension docs)

And that's indeed how the observable behavior worked on earlier Julia versions, including 1.11.0. It sometimes shows up during Github CI testing of our simplified `crash` branch:
```
pkg> add Sunny#crash
pkg> test Sunny
```

It only crashes sometimes, however. On my Mac, for example, crashes are rare, but when they happen, it's in roughly the same code location. An example of the segfault output is shown from this CI action: https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/actions/runs/4214225988/jobs/7314550112

The segfault seems to always occur inside FFTW, but perhaps there is memory corruption happening prior to FFTW.

The branch `Sunny#crash` contains no `@inbounds` annotations, or other "memory unsafe" operations from what we can tell (presumably the FFT package is intended to be memory safe?). Sunny does depend on external C libraries, which could of course corrupt memory.

I tried to bisect to a commit where the crash first appeared, and it seems to be one of these two:
https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/commit/fb0a63195a0afdf4c0c58260f36fed098d263a85 <- where crashes become very noticeable
https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/commit/9f97b542450e7c038db2f3e575c83c78d801a37e <- parent commit, seems suspicious to me

It sometimes shows up during Github CI testing of our simplified `crash` branch:
```
pkg> add Sunny#crash
pkg> test Sunny
```

It only crashes sometimes, however. On my Mac, for example, crashes are rare, but when they happen, it's in roughly the same code location. An example of the segfault output is shown from this CI action: https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/actions/runs/4214225988/jobs/7314550112

The segfault seems to always occur inside FFTW, but perhaps there is memory corruption happening prior to FFTW.

The branch `Sunny#crash` contains no `@inbounds` annotations, or other "memory unsafe" operations from what we can tell (presumably the FFT package is intended to be memory safe?). Sunny does depend on external C libraries, which could of course corrupt memory.

I tried to bisect to a commit where the crash first appeared, and it seems to be one of these two:
https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/commit/fb0a63195a0afdf4c0c58260f36fed098d263a85 <- where crashes become very noticeable
https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/commit/9f97b542450e7c038db2f3e575c83c78d801a37e <- parent commit, seems suspicious to me

It sometimes shows up during Github CI testing of our simplified `crash` branch:
```

```

It only crashes sometimes, however. On my Mac, for example, crashes are rare, but when they happen, it's in roughly the same code location. An example of the segfault output is shown from this CI action: https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/actions/runs/4214225988/jobs/7314550112

The segfault seems to always occur inside FFTW, but perhaps there is memory corruption happening prior to FFTW.

https://github.com/SunnySuite/Sunny.jl/commit/fb0a63195a0afdf4c0c58260f36fed098d263a85 <- where crashes become very noticeable
